howItWorks.intro: This rainwater harvest calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Volume = Roof Area × Rainfall Depth × 7.48 gal/cu ft
1 inch of rain on 1,000 sq ft ≈ 623 gallons
85% efficiency accounts for gutter losses and first-flush diversion
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much rainwater can I collect?
faq.answer: About 600 gallons per 1,000 sq ft of roof per inch of rain. A 1,500 sq ft roof with 35 inches of annual rainfall can collect about 27,000 gallons per year — enough for a significant portion of landscape irrigation.

faq.question: Is rainwater harvesting legal?
faq.answer: Most US states allow it. Colorado previously restricted it but now allows up to two 55-gallon barrels per household. Some states actively encourage it with tax credits. Check your state and local regulations.

faq.question: What can I use harvested rainwater for?
faq.answer: Garden irrigation is the most common use. With proper filtration and treatment, it can be used for flushing toilets, laundry, and even drinking water. Untreated rainwater should not be used for drinking or cooking.
